title: Sweet Tender Ethiopian Duck Stir-Fry
description: A sweet and tender duck dish that is perfect for a crowd-pleaser dinner option, gluten-free and kosher-friendly.
instructions: Season duck breasts with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add duck breasts skin-side down, searing for about 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown.
Remove duck from skillet and set aside. In the same skillet, add sliced peppers and minced garlic, sautéing for about 1 minute or until softened slightly.
Add grated ginger to the skillet and cook for another minute, stirring frequently.
Stir in flour and honey, cooking for a few seconds until fragrant.

Pour in chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Stir in cumin and red pepper flakes (if using). Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for about 20 minutes or until thickened slightly.
Return duck breasts to skillet, spooning sauce over them evenly. Cook for an additional 15-20 minutes or until the internal temperature of the duck reaches at least 165°F (74°C).
Transfer duck and sauce to a serving dish and let rest for about 5 minutes.
Serve hot with steamed vegetables on the side.
